title: Hattie the Mouse and the Rainy Day Stove Rescue
summary: When Hattie the Mouse finds her stove broken on a rainy day, she calls upon her Nana, a bunny named Peter Rabbit, for assistance. Peter Rabbit visits over the weekend and discovers the cause of the problem: a spoon had fallen behind the appliance. Once the stove is fixed and plugged back in, the two celebrate their success by preparing a feast of pasta and a fresh garden salad.
audioFilename: * Stars and Stories - Hattie’s Nana Fixes the Stove 2.m4a

highlightQuote.text: She's a bunny and her name is Peter Rabbit.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Hattie the Mouse had a big adventure going to the swimming pool.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, it was raining.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was raining. So what did Hattie the Mouse do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She stayed at home.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She stayed at home. And what did she do in her house?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She just took a nap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She just took a nap. And when she woke up she had a big problem. What was the problem?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Her stove was not working.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, her stove was not working. What a big problem. She tried to turn it on and click, click, click, it wouldn't light so she couldn't cook her food. So what did she do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She called her Nana.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She called her Nana. Hattie the Mouse picked up her phone and went boop-boop-boop to dial her Nana's number and went bring-bring-bring. And then her Nana answered. Who's Hattie's Nana?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She's a bunny and her name is Peter Rabbit.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie's Nana is Peter Rabbit. Okay. And so Peter Rabbit answered and said, "Oh, hello Hattie. How are you today?" and what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I got a big problem.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh dear, what's the problem? said Peter.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's my stove doesn't not work. Could you please come over this weekend?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Peter Rabbit said, "Well, yes, of course I'd be happy to come over and help anytime. I'll do anything for you Hattie. Okay, I'll come over on the weekend, see you then." And then that weekend there was a knock on Hattie's door. And who was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nana!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was Peter Rabbit Nana coming over to Hattie's house. And Peter Rabbit Nana came in and said, "Oh, let's look at your stove together. Let's see. Click, click, click. Oh, it doesn't light. There must be some kind of problem." And what did Peter do next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He said hmm... and then oh Nana Peter Rabbit... a spoon fell behind.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A spoon fell behind the stove said Hattie the Mouse. Maybe that's what did it. And what did Peter do to fix it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She got it out and plugged it back in and then Hattie could cook her food.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said, "Oh, let's try it again." Click, phew! It lit the stove and now she could cook some delicious food and she said, "Oh, thank you Nana. Let's cook a meal together to celebrate." And so what did they cook?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Pasta.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They cooked some delicious pasta. And what else did they have with their pasta?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some cucumber and carrot and they had a big salad.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that sounds like a delicious meal. And so Hattie the Mouse and her Nana Peter Rabbit had a delicious meal to celebrate fixing the stove. And that's the end of the story.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Peter Rabbit
characters.description: Every Sunday, Hattie’s Nana—a diligent bunny with a mechanical mind—arrives to spearhead ambitious backyard cleaning projects. Whether she is hauling brush or recalibrating a temperamental stove with her steady paws, her tireless spirit and technical handiness make her an indispensable force of domestic restoration.

places.name: Hattie's House
places.description: Nestled in a sun-drenched tree across from the town park, Hattie’s arboreal sanctuary on Mouse Street doubles as a vibrant Cornell Street workshop for designing hats and a peaceful retreat for resting after long days of painting. This cozy home, cluttered with the tools of her trade, is a place of shared morning breakfasts with Mischief and savory meals cooked alongside her Nana. Whether lulled by the rhythmic drumming of rainfall or the quiet exhaustion of her artistry, Hattie finds her solace here, drifting into restorative naps amidst the creative clutter.
